metadata.method: Tavily search of justice.gov press releases
metadata.notes: 40% floor — true prosecution rate is 100% for 1128a1 (conviction required). Many cases prosecuted at state level or via plea deals without DOJ press releases. CA (0/7) and OH (1/9) have low federal match rates suggesting state-level prosecution.

matches.name: Hal Abrahamson
matches.summary: Sentenced 1 year and 1 day for healthcare fraud. Billed for skin grafts never performed, billed under another podiatrist's name.

matches.name: Darrell Bryant
matches.summary: Convicted Dec 2018, conspiracy to commit health care fraud. Sentenced to 84 months.

matches.name: Zamika Walls
matches.summary: Sentenced 15 months for $2.5M Medicaid fraud scheme in Missouri. Listed as FL in LEIE.

matches.name: Andrew Bohlman
matches.summary: Pleaded guilty to Medicaid fraud in Montana. Listed as AZ in LEIE.

matches.name: Jimmie Anthony
matches.summary: Physical therapist pleaded guilty to billing Medicare for PT services through West Shore Comfort home care company.

matches.name: Mark Zager
matches.summary: Charged with conspiracy to commit health care fraud and wire fraud in Southern District of Florida.

matches.name: Riyaz Mazkouri
matches.summary: Indicted as part of 2016 Medicare Fraud Strike Force takedown. Attending physician at psychiatric hospital in Harris County, TX.

matches.name: Andrzej Zielke
matches.summary: Former Pittsburgh-area doctor pleaded guilty to unlawfully prescribing opioids and health care fraud via fraudulent Medicaid claims.

matches.name: Syed Aziz
matches.summary: Part of $60 million Medicare fraud scheme in Northern District of Texas. 16 individuals charged.

matches.name: Eva Gateva
matches.summary: Named in Zemlyansky et al. indictment — $110M mail fraud conspiracy in Eastern District of New York.

matches.name: Moses Degraft-Johnson
matches.summary: Dual US-Ghana citizen pleaded guilty to $2.9M health insurance fraud and conspiracy in Northern District of Florida.

matches.name: Ahmed El Soury
matches.summary: Sentenced to 33 months for role in test referral scheme at New Jersey clinical lab.

matches.name: Salman Ali
matches.summary: Part of $34M Detroit-area Medicare Fraud Strike Force takedown. 20 residents charged.

matches.name: Hector Molina
matches.summary: Part of largest national Medicare fraud takedown in history. Charged in Dallas, Northern District of Texas.

matches.name: Kyrenia Rodriguez
matches.summary: Physical therapy assistant sentenced to 30 months for medical billing fraud in South Florida.

matches.name: Jason Gerner
matches.summary: Sentenced to 3 years and $9M restitution for drug rehab health care fraud scheme.

matches.name: Suhyun An
matches.summary: Chiropractor agreed to $2.6M settlement and 10-year exclusion for fraudulent billing of acupuncture devices.
